text.title: BA Week-One Pack Output Contract
text.summary: Pins the shape of the deliverable. A BA who passes Day-5 review has produced exactly this folder structure with exactly these required fields populated. Anything else is "not done."

section.title: Required deliverable
section.p: The output of week one is a folder named week-one-pack/ committed to the project workspace (Confluence space, Notion page tree, Drive folder, or git repo — the BA picks one and stays there).

list.item: README.md — project name, BA name, week range, sign-off field with engagement-manager name + date.
inventory.md — flat artefact list per r1.
stakeholders.md — table per r2; >= 1 row with Approve authority for the primary business outcome.
glossary.md — >= 15 terms per r3; contested flag column present.
processes.md — >= 1 swimlane/BPMN diagram per r4; lanes tied to stakeholder names.
risks.md — top 5 gaps, top 5 risks (L x I), open questions per r5.

antipattern.description: Required file present but empty or template-default content.
antipattern.reason: Counts as not-written and silently passes a casual review. Validator must reject on byte-count and template-string detection.

antipattern.description: Stakeholders table with any "TBD" in the Authority or Name column.
antipattern.reason: Unknown authority is the same as missing the stakeholder for routing decisions.

antipattern.description: Processes.md contains only prose, no diagram, even if linked-out to an external Lucidchart.
antipattern.reason: External links break with seat changes and lose handoff context. Diagram must be committed as PNG/SVG inside the pack folder.

antipattern.description: Risks.md exists but lacks a written sign-off line from the engagement manager.
antipattern.reason: The sign-off is the protection mechanism. Without it the gap memo cannot be referenced in later scope disputes.

list.item: Stakeholder authority may use RAPID labels (R/A/P/I/D) instead of Approve/Consult/Inform/None — must be consistent across the file.
Glossary may include up to 5 cross-references to existing org glossaries instead of inlining definitions, provided the source URL and last-checked date are recorded.
Processes.md may include multiple diagrams if the as-is process splits; r4 minimum is 1.
All six required files exist with non-template content.
Stakeholders.md has >= 1 Approve-authority row matching the SOW primary outcome.
Glossary count >= 15, contested flag present and used at least once OR a justified note that no terms were contested.
Processes.md has a diagram file (PNG/SVG) and lane labels match stakeholder names.
Risks.md has 5+5 minimum rows and a sign-off line with name + ISO date.
README.md sign-off block filled by engagement manager, not by the BA.
